title: Gateway OOM crash loop (TKT-0040)
cause: Node.js heap OOM: sessions.json grew to 16MB+ (1.7GB total sessions dir), exhausting default Node.js heap. Gateway crashed 8 times over 5 days with FATAL ERROR 'Reached heap limit Allocation failed'. Secondary issue: obs-collector.sh timezone parsing bug caused same error log line to be re-detected on every 5-min run, inflating 8 real restarts into 235+ obs events.
resolution: 1. Added NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 to LaunchAgent plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist (activates on next gateway restart). 2. Fixed line_epoch() timezone parsing bug in scripts/obs-collector.sh — local +10:00 timestamps were treated as UTC, making log lines appear 10h in the future and thus always newer than lastRunEpoch. 3. Cleaned 5 stale .tmp session files from ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/. 4. Reset obs-collector lastRunEpoch to prevent re-detection of old error log lines.
impact: Gateway auto-restarted 8 times, interrupting active main sessions. 235+ false-positive gateway_restart obs events generated, masking real signal.
actionRequired: Ken: restart gateway at next convenient time to activate NODE_OPTIONS heap increase. Command: openclaw gateway restart
